story: The radiology department reviewed one hundred and fifty chest X-rays for the month. Fifty-two showed no significant abnormalities. Nineteen were flagged for follow-up CT scans based on protocol criteria. The remainder were classified as normal variants or age-appropriate changes. All reports were sent to the referring physicians within twenty-four hours. The chief radiologist held a weekly conference to review ambiguous cases. A quality assurance audit found documentation compliance at ninety-eight percent. Staff attended a training session on new imaging protocols. Equipment calibration was performed on schedule. Patient volumes were consistent with projections.
